response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has genuine geospatial and distributed-systems exposure, but the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust systems programming — is not met. His C++ work was limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a few academic/hobby projects; his entire post-2016 career is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of routing pipeline work, navigation tile generation, or the low-level systems programming depth this mid-senior role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS experience with real deployed products (GISCollective, Green Map GIS app used in production by NGOs globally)
Proven ability to build high-throughput distributed systems — Dunnhumby work targeted millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and low-latency design
Demonstrated range across domains (embedded, mobile, web, infrastructure) and consistent pattern of owning unfamiliar problem spaces
concerns: C++ experience is both minimal (roughly 6 months at NI in 2014–2015) and dated; no Rust experience anywhere on the resume
No routing pipeline, navigation tile processing, or geospatial data transformation work — the specific domain expertise the role is built around
Primary commercial expertise for the past 8+ years is TypeScript/Node.js web development, which is a significant mismatch with a systems-level C++ IC role
